channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: 'Must-see event', rare sumo tournament kicks off in Paris
item.description: A 150-strong delegation, two chartered planes... More than 30 years after their last visit to France, the elite of sumo wrestling – Japan's national and sacred sport – is making a highly anticipated return to Paris for a weekend showcase.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: EU says to resume membership talks with Ukraine on Monday
item.description: The European Union will resume membership talks with Ukraine on Monday, the bloc announced, after Hungary lifted the veto imposed by its previous pro-Russian leader.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: G7 meeting begins in France this Monday
item.description: U.S. ​President ​Donald Trump will meet with Middle ​Eastern ‌leaders ⁠and ‌attend a working session ⁠with Ukrainian President ​Volodymyr Zelenskiy ‌during the G7 summit ‌in France ​next week, senior U.S. administration officials ​said.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Switzerland: After rapid population growth, right-wing party supports cap vote
item.description:
Swiss voters will decide the fate of a divisive anti-immigration proposal on Sunday to cap the country's population, and another referendum on restricting access to conscientious objection to military service.
France 24's John Zarocostas tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US: New York Knicks win first NBA championship since 1973
item.description: The New York Knicks are champions of the NBA for the first time since 1973, beating the San Antonio Spurs in five games for this title. The clincher came Saturday night in a 94-90 victory, the Knicks' fourth comeback win of the series.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Colombia leftist candidate says ready to overhaul peace process
item.description: Colombia's left-wing presidential hopeful Ivan Cepeda said he was open to making changes to the much-criticized peace process launched by outgoing President Gustavo Petro, his staunchest ally.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Thousands of protesters expected in Geneva ahead of G7 summit
item.description: On the eve of the G7 summit in Evian, France, Geneva is expecting thousands of protesters to converge on the city on Sunday, under close police surveillance, more than 20 years after the violence that marred the 2003 G8 summit. The "No-G7" coalition of associations, unions, and left-wing groups says it aims to denounce "fascism and imperialism".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Thousands rally in Belfast to condemn anti-immigrant rioting that followed stabbing
item.description: Thousands of demonstrators gathered in Belfast on Saturday to denounce anti-immigrant rioters who set fire to homes and cars earlier in the week after a brutal stabbing blamed on an asylum seeker.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Swiss cast ballots on right-wing's bid to cap country's population at 10 million
item.description: Voters in Switzerland are casting final ballots Sunday on an initiative championed by the top right-wing party to cap the rich Alpine country's population at 10 million.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran: US, ​Pakistani leaders predict Sunday signing of peace framework
item.description: U.S. and Pakistani leaders forecast a Sunday signing of a long-elusive framework agreement to end months of fighting between the United States and Iran, but Tehran cast doubt over the timing and hardline protesters in Iran voiced opposition.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: UK intercepts oil tanker from Russia's shadow fleet in English Channel
item.description: British forces intercepted a UK-sanctioned oil tanker accused of belonging to what has been dubbed Russia's "shadow fleet" in the English Channel on Sunday, in what the country’s defence ministry called "the first UK-led operation of its kind".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Australia stun Turkey after selection gamble pays off
item.description: Australia defeated Turkey 2-0 on Saturday at Vancouver's BC Place Stadium thanks to a goal in each half by Nestory Irankunda and Connor Metcalfe.
item.source: © Stu Forster, Getty Images via AFP
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: History's first influencer? Marie Antoinette still receives letters
item.description: Two hundred and forty years after their last stay at the Château de Fontainebleau south of Paris, France's King Louis XVI and Queen Marie Antoinette are again taking centre stage there. Some 4,000 letters have been sent to the late queen under the chateau’s “Write to Marie‑Antoinette” initiative, showing that she in particular is still a subject of fascination to the public.
item.dc:creator: Stéphanie TROUILLARD

item.title: Middle East war live: Trump says Iran deal to be signed Sunday
item.description: US and Pakistani leaders predicted a Sunday signing of an agreement to end the war against Iran, with US President Donald Trump saying the critical Strait of Hormuz would be "open to all" immediately after. Iranian foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei had said earlier on Saturday that the date of the signing was yet to be determined, but that it would not be on Sunday. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: New York Knicks beat Spurs to claim first NBA title in 53 years
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: New York Knicks beat Spurs to claim first NBA title in 53 years
item.description: Jalen Brunson scored 45 points as the New York Knicks beat the San Antonio Spurs 94-90 on Saturday to clinch their first NBA championship in 53 years. The Knicks sealed the best-of-seven NBA Finals 4-1, taking down French star Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs on their home court to capture their first title since 1973.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Scotland edge past Haiti with McGinn's opener
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Scotland edge past Haiti with McGinn's opener
item.description: Scotland secured a narrow 1-0 victory over Haiti in their Group C opener at Boston's stadium. John McGinn struck early in the 28th minute after a chaotic scramble in the box, before Haiti mounted sustained pressure but lacked the clinical finishing to break down Scotland's resolute defence.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Despite regular strikes on Ukraine, Russia's offensive seems to be losing steam
item.description: Strikes on Ukraine remain a regular occurrence, but behind this show of force, the Russian army’s offensive on the front lines is showing signs of slowing down, analysts say. More than four years after the full-scale invasion began, Russian capture of Ukrainian towns are becoming increasingly rare.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Switzerland holds Brexit-style referendum on proposed 10 million population cap
item.description: Swiss voters will decide on Sunday whether to approve a proposal to cap the country's population at 10 million by 2050, a measure critics say could have Brexit-like consequences for the economy and relations with the European Union. The referendum reflects growing anti-immigration sentiment seen across Europe.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Brazil and Morocco draw in heavyweight Group C opener
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Brazil and Morocco draw in heavyweight Group C opener
item.description: Brazil and Morocco played out a 1-1 draw in their Group C clash at MetLife stadium, the venue that will host World Cup final. Saibari's stunning lob gave Morocco an early lead, but Vinicius's superb finish dragged Brazil level before half-time, setting up a tense second half that saw both sides trade chances.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Saibari and Vinicius spark draw in Group C drama
item.description: Brazil and Morocco drew 1-1 in their Group C opener at MetLife stadium. Saibari's early lob gave Morocco the lead, but Vinicius Junior's sublime finish levelled the match before half-time. Both sides traded chances in a tense second half, neither able to break the deadlock. Read our blog to see how the match unfolded.
item.dc:creator: Mehdi BOUZOUINA

item.title: World Cup 2026: Qatar stuns Switzerland with late equaliser
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
